title: Per-disease extension pattern, validated on one exemplar subtype
context: The CDE set is designed as a minimal core plus optional modules, with per-disease specialisation demonstrated on at most one or two exemplar subtypes rather than built exhaustively for all rare cancers. Once a community has adopted the standards, the extension pattern should be documented and validated so other communities can specialise without duplicating effort or diverging from the shared core. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-adopt-001. Standards reviewer and oncologist reviewer sign-off required.
objective: Document and validate the per-disease extension pattern (`docs/extension-pattern.md` + exemplar extension in `extensions/`): (1) describe the LinkML pattern for creating a disease-specific extension to the minimal core, (2) specify how extension CDEs inherit or override core fields, bind to disease-specific ontologies, and carry provenance, (3) validate the pattern on one exemplar rare cancer (e.g., a sarcoma subtype or ultra-rare tumour with mature open ontology coverage) using synthetic fixtures, and (4) confirm the exemplar extension passes CI without requiring changes to the core.
acceptanceCriteria: Extension pattern documented: how to add disease-specific CDEs in LinkML without modifying the minimal core; inheritance and override rules specified.
Exemplar extension created for one named rare-cancer subtype (decided with the adopting community) using open-vocabulary bindings and synthetic fixtures.
Exemplar extension carries 100% provenance per the prov-001 model; CI provenance linter and licence deny-list linter pass.
The exemplar round-trips through the crosswalk (to mCODE/OMOP/Phenopackets) using the synthetic test-bank; CI tests pass.
Core CDE set is unchanged by the extension (extension is additive only).
`pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green; standards reviewer and oncologist sign-off recorded.
output: A `docs/extension-pattern.md` guide and a `extensions/<exemplar-subtype>/` LinkML extension with synthetic fixtures, committed via PR with standards reviewer and oncologist sign-off.
